Each character is chosen with extraordinary care — not just for meaning, but for its sound, visual beauty, and cultural resonance."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["The finest names draw from China's greatest literary works: the ",["$","em",null,{"children":"Book of Songs"}]," (诗经, circa 1000 BC), the ",["$","em",null,{"children":"Analects"}]," (论语), the ",["$","em",null,{"children":"Tao Te Ching"}]," (道德经), the poetry of Du Fu and Li Bai, and the classical ",["$","em",null,{"children":"Chu Ci"}]," (楚辞). A single character can carry centuries of cultural meaning."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["For example, ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"浩然"}],"(Hào Rán) comes directly from Mencius: “I am skilled at nurturing my vast, righteous spirit” — two characters that encode a complete philosophy of moral cultivation."]}]]}]]}]

8:["$","section",null,{"className":"bg-amber-50/80 backdrop-blur-sm rounded-2xl p-8 border border-amber-200","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-xl font-bold text-stone-800 mb-4","children":"A Note on Cultural Respect"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-stone-600 leading-relaxed","children":"Chinese naming is a tradition that spans thousands of years and carries profound cultural significance. We approach this art with deep respect for the culture and literature that inform it. Every name we generate is grounded in authentic classical sources, and we strive to create names that a native Chinese speaker would recognize as beautiful, meaningful, and appropriate — not mere phonetic approximations or superficial translations."}]]}]
